client_like.h(3) Library Functions Manual client_like.h(3)

NAME

client_like.h - Shared initialization and teardown for client-like modes (mirror, client, discovery).

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct session_client_like_config

Typedefs


typedef enum session_client_like_kind session_client_like_kind_t
typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_client_like_run_fn) (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, void *user_data)
typedef struct session_client_like_config session_client_like_config_t

Enumerations


enum session_client_like_kind { SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR = 0, SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT, SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY }

Functions


terminal_fd_reader_t * session_client_like_get_stdin_reader (void)
asciichat_error_t session_client_like_run (const session_client_like_config_t *config)

Variables


bool(*)(void *) session_client_like_get_render_should_exit (void)

Detailed Description

Shared initialization and teardown for client-like modes (mirror, client, discovery).

Provides a unified interface for running client-like modes that share common initialization patterns: media source selection, FPS probing, audio initialization, display creation, splash screen management, keepawake handling, and cleanup.

Each mode registers mode-specific callbacks to handle initialization, the main render loop, and keyboard input. The shared layer handles all the boilerplate.

Modes Supported

  • Mirror mode: Local webcam/media playback without networking
  • Client mode: Network client with per-connection initialization
  • Discovery mode: P2P mode with role negotiation

Shared Responsibilities

This layer automatically handles:

  • Terminal output management (stdout piping detection)
  • Keepawake system (platform sleep prevention)
  • Splash screen lifecycle and animation
  • Media source selection and FPS probing
  • Audio initialization and lifecycle
  • Display context creation and management
  • Render loop execution
  • Proper cleanup ordering (critical for PortAudio)

Mode-Specific Responsibilities

Mode files provide:

  • Main loop callback (run_fn) that calls session_render_loop() or custom loop
  • Optional network interrupt callback for socket shutdown
  • Optional custom exit condition for additional shutdown criteria
  • Keyboard handler for interactive controls
  • Terminal behavior preferences (newline on exit)

Memory and Lifecycle

All allocations (capture, display, audio) are owned and cleaned up by session_client_like_run(). Mode callbacks receive initialized, ready-to-use contexts and should not attempt to free them.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file client_like.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct session_client_like_config session_client_like_config_t

Configuration for session_client_like_run()

All media, display, audio, FPS, and snapshot settings are read automatically from GET_OPTION() inside the function. Callers only provide the fields below that differ per mode.

typedef enum session_client_like_kind session_client_like_kind_t

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_client_like_run_fn) (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, void *user_data)

Mode-specific main loop callback

Called after all shared initialization is complete (capture opened, audio started, display ready, splash done). The mode runs its main loop here and returns when finished. The shared teardown runs after this returns.

Typical pattern:

// For mirror/discovery participant:
return session_render_loop(capture, display, exit_check_fn, NULL, NULL,


                           keyboard_handler, user_data);
// For client mode (protocol-driven):
while (!should_exit()) {


  // protocol loop...
}
return ASCIICHAT_OK;

Parameters

capture Initialized capture context. Never NULL.
display Initialized display context. Never NULL.
user_data User data passed through from config.run_user_data

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK, or error code to abort (cleanup still runs)

Definition at line 98 of file client_like.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum session_client_like_kind

Enumerator

SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR
SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT
SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY

Definition at line 63 of file client_like.h.

63                                       {
64   SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR = 0,
65   SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT,
66   SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY,
67 } session_client_like_kind_t;










Function
  Documentation




asciichat_error_t session_client_like_run (const
  session_client_like_config_t * config)


Run a client-like mode with fully shared initialization and
    teardown




This function orchestrates the complete lifecycle of client-like
    modes:








Initialization
  (automatic from GET_OPTION())


Terminal:


    
  
  • Forces stderr when stdout is piped (terminal_should_force_stderr)
    • 
  
  • Disables terminal logging during rendering, re-enables on exit
    • 
  
  • Handles snapshot mode log visibility
    • 



Keepawake:


    
  
  • Validates --keepawake / --no-keepawake mutual exclusivity
    • 
  
  • Calls platform_enable_keepawake() unless --no-keepawake set
    • 
  
  • Disables keepawake on exit
    • 



Splash Screen:


    
  
  • Creates temporary display context
    • 
  
  • Calls splash_intro_start() to show splash animation
    • 
  
  • Holds splash visible during media probing and audio init
    • 
  
  • Calls splash_intro_done() when capture and display are ready
    • 



Media / Webcam
    (session_capture_ctx_t):



  
•

  
Selects source type from options (priority order):





  
 1.

  
--url → MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE (FFmpeg handles http/rtsp/rtmp)

  
 2.

  
--file → MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE; "-" →
      MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN

  
 3.

  
--test-pattern → MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST

  
 4.

  
(default) → MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM




    
  
  • FPS: uses GET_OPTION(fps) if > 0, otherwise probes with
      temporary media source
    • 
  
  • Applies GET_OPTION(media_seek_timestamp) as initial seek
    • 
  
  • Applies GET_OPTION(media_loop) for file looping
    • 
  
  • Calls session_capture_create() to open webcam or media file
    • 
  
  • Destroys capture on exit
    • 



Audio (audio_context_t):


    
  
  • Skipped entirely for immediate snapshots (snapshot_mode &&
      snapshot_delay == 0)
    • 
  
  • Probes for audio via media_source_has_audio() on capture's
    source
    • 
  
  • Allocates and initializes audio_context_t when audio available
    • 
  
  • Links audio context to capture and media source
    • 
  
  • Sets playback_only based on audio_should_enable_microphone()
    • 
  
  • Disables jitter buffering for local file playback
    • 
  
  • Calls audio_start_duplex() after splash_intro_done()
    • 
  
  • Stops, destroys, and frees audio on exit
    • 



Display (session_display_ctx_t):


    
  
  • Calls session_display_create() with snapshot and color
    settings
    • 
  
  • Passes audio context if initialized
    • 
  
  • Destroys display on exit
    • 







Mode-Specific
  Loop


Calls config->run_fn(capture, display,
    config->run_user_data) once all initialization is complete. This is where
    each mode calls session_render_loop() or runs its own
    protocol-thread-driven loop.








Cleanup
  (always runs, even on error)


Cleanup sequence (order is critical):



  
1.

  
Re-enable terminal logging

  
2.

  
audio_terminate_portaudio_final() - Free PortAudio device resources
      FIRST

  
3.

  
audio_stop_duplex() + audio_destroy() + SAFE_FREE

  
4.

  
session_display_destroy()

  
5.

  
session_capture_destroy()

  
6.

  
webcam_destroy() - Free cached webcam images and test patterns

  
7.

  
session_log_buffer_destroy()

  
8.

  
platform_disable_keepawake()

  
9.

  
Print '
    

  




Parameters


Config Module Mode configuration.
  Must not be NULL. run_fn must not be NULL.


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, or the first error code from
  initialization or from run_fn. Cleanup always runs regardless of return
  value.


Definition at line 95 of file client_like.c.


95                                                                                       {
96   log_info("session_client_like_run: ENTRY");
97   if (!config || !config->run_fn) {
98     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config or run_fn is NULL");
99   }
100   log_info("session_client_like_run: config validated");
101 
102   // Store config globally for adapter callbacks
103   g_current_config = config;
104 
105   // Store the render should_exit adapter globally so mode-specific run_fn can access it
106   g_render_should_exit = display_should_exit_adapter;
107 
108   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
109 
110   // Keep track of what's been initialized for cleanup
111   session_capture_ctx_t *capture = NULL;
112   session_display_ctx_t *display = NULL;
113   audio_context_t *audio_ctx = NULL;
114   media_source_t *probe_source = NULL;
115   bool audio_available = false;
116 
117   // ============================================================================
118   // SETUP: Terminal and Logging
119   // ============================================================================
120 
121   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Setting up terminal and logging");
122 
123   // Force stderr when stdout is piped (prevents ASCII corruption in output stream)
124   bool should_force_stderr = terminal_should_force_stderr();
125   log_debug("terminal_should_force_stderr()=%d", should_force_stderr);
126 
127   if (should_force_stderr) {
128     // Redirect logs to stderr to prevent corruption of stdout (for pipes)
129     // But keep terminal output enabled so we can see initialization errors
130     log_set_force_stderr(true);
131   }
132 
133   // Note: Force all logging to stderr if encoding to pipe to prevent corrupting binary video output
134   // (implemented via should_force_stderr check above)
135 #ifndef _WIN32
136   const char *render_file_early = GET_OPTION(render_file);
137   log_info("[CLIENT_LIKE_RUN] render_file_early=%s (ptr=%p)",
138            render_file_early ? render_file_early : "(null)", (void *)render_file_early);
139   if (render_file_early && render_file_early[0] != '\0' &&
140       (strcmp(render_file_early, "-") == 0 || strcmp(render_file_early, "pipe:") == 0)) {
141     log_info("--render-file=- will output binary video to stdout, logging sent to stderr");
142   }
143 #endif
144 
145   // ============================================================================
146   // SETUP: Keepawake System
147   // ============================================================================
148 
149   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Validating keepawake options");
150 
151   // Validate mutual exclusivity
152   bool enable_ka = GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake);
153   bool disable_ka = GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake);
154   log_debug("enable_keepawake=%d, disable_keepawake=%d", enable_ka, disable_ka);
155 
156   if (enable_ka && disable_ka) {
157     result = SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "--keepawake and --no-keepawake are mutually exclusive");
158     goto cleanup;
159   }
160 
161   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Enabling keepawake if needed");
162 
163   // Enable keepawake unless explicitly disabled
164   if (!disable_ka) {
165     (void)platform_enable_keepawake();
166   }
167 
168   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Keepawake setup complete");
169 
170   // ============================================================================
171   // SETUP: Display Context (before splash for unified TTY initialization)
172   // ============================================================================
173 
174   // Create display context early to share TTY initialization between splash and rendering
175   // This prevents double TTY init which was corrupting the terminal state
176   session_display_config_t display_config = {0};
177   display_config.snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
178   display_config.palette_type = GET_OPTION(palette_type);
179   display_config.custom_palette = GET_OPTION(palette_custom_set) ? GET_OPTION(palette_custom) : NULL;
180   display_config.color_mode = TERM_COLOR_AUTO;
181   display_config.should_exit_callback = display_should_exit_adapter;
182   display_config.callback_data = NULL;
183 
184   log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] Creating display context (before splash)");
185   display = session_display_create(&display_config);
186   if (!display) {
187     log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] session_display_create() returned NULL");
188     log_fatal("Failed to initialize display context");
189     result = ERROR_DISPLAY;
190     goto cleanup;
191   }
192   log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] Display context created, will use for splash");
193 
194   // Register global display context for signal handlers (Ctrl+C help screen closure)
195   // This makes the context available to signal handlers for the entire app lifetime
196   session_display_set_global_context(display);
197 
198   // ============================================================================
199   // SETUP: Splash Screen (using unified display context)
200   // ============================================================================
201 
202   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Starting splash with display context");
203 
204   // Use the display context we just created for splash animation
205   splash_intro_start(display);
206   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): splash_intro_start() returned");
207 
208   // Detect if we're using media vs webcam (needed for splash timing)
209   const char *media_url = GET_OPTION(media_url);
210   const char *media_file = GET_OPTION(media_file);
211   bool has_media = (media_url && strlen(media_url) > 0) || (media_file && strlen(media_file) > 0);
212 
213   // Show splash briefly for webcam, but skip sleep for media (which takes time anyway)
214   if (!has_media && !GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
215     APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
216     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT(platform_sleep_ms, 250);
217   }
218   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): After splash sleep");
219 
220   // Restore stderr now that splash animation and post-splash logging are done
221   // This allows logs to appear on screen again after this point
222   splash_restore_stderr();
223 
224   // ============================================================================
225   // SETUP: Terminal Logging in Snapshot Mode
226   // ============================================================================
227 
228   // ============================================================================
229   // SETUP: Terminal Logging in Snapshot Mode
230   // ============================================================================
231 
232   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): About to disable terminal logging (snapshot=%d, discovery=%d)", GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode), config->discovery != NULL);
233   // For discovery mode, skip disabling logging initially - we'll handle it after role is determined
234   // Discovery participants should show logs, only hosts should have logs disabled for rendering
235   bool should_disable_logging = !GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && (config->discovery == NULL);
236   if (should_disable_logging) {
237     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Calling log_set_terminal_output(false)...");
238     log_set_terminal_output(false);
239     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): RETURNED from log_set_terminal_output(false)");
240   } else {
241     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Skipping log disable (snapshot=%d or discovery=%d)", GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode), config->discovery != NULL);
242   }
243   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Terminal logging setup complete, starting media source setup");
244 
245   // ============================================================================
246   // SETUP: Media Source Selection and FPS Probing
247   // ============================================================================
248 
249   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Initializing capture config");
250   session_capture_config_t capture_config = {0};
251   capture_config.resize_for_network = false;
252   capture_config.should_exit_callback = capture_should_exit_adapter;
253   capture_config.callback_data = NULL;
254 
255   // Determine FPS explicitly set by user
256   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting FPS option");
257   int user_fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
258   bool fps_explicitly_set = user_fps > 0;
259   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): FPS=%d (explicitly_set=%d)", user_fps, fps_explicitly_set);
260 
261   // Select media source based on options (priority order)
262   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_url option");
263   const char *media_url_val = GET_OPTION(media_url);
264   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_file option");
265   const char *media_file_val = GET_OPTION(media_file);
266   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): media_url=%s, media_file=%s", media_url_val ? media_url_val : "(null)",
267             media_file_val ? media_file_val : "(null)");
268 
269   if (media_url_val && strlen(media_url_val) > 0) {
270     // Network URL takes priority
271     log_info("Using network URL: %s (webcam disabled)", media_url_val);
272     capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
273     capture_config.path = media_url_val;
274     capture_config.loop = false; // Network URLs cannot be looped
275 
276     if (fps_explicitly_set) {
277       capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)user_fps;
278       log_info("Using user-specified FPS: %u", capture_config.target_fps);
279     } else {
280       // Always probe FPS for HTTP URLs, even in snapshot mode
281       // (snapshot mode needs correct FPS to capture right number of frames during snapshot_delay window)
282       log_debug("Probing FPS for HTTP URL");
283       probe_source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, media_url_val);
284       if (probe_source) {
285         double url_fps = media_source_get_video_fps(probe_source);
286         log_info("Detected HTTP stream video FPS: %.1f", url_fps);
287         if (url_fps > 0.0) {
288           capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)(url_fps + 0.5);
289         } else {
290           log_warn("FPS detection failed for HTTP stream, using default 60 FPS");
291           capture_config.target_fps = 60;
292         }
293       } else {
294         log_warn("Failed to create probe source for HTTP stream, using default 60 FPS");
295         capture_config.target_fps = 60;
296       }
297     }
298   } else if (media_file_val && strlen(media_file_val) > 0) {
299     // Local file or stdin
300     log_info("DEBUG: media_file_val='%s', strcmp result=%d", media_file_val, strcmp(media_file_val, "-"));
301     if (strcmp(media_file_val, "-") == 0) {
302       log_info("Using stdin for media streaming (webcam disabled)");
303       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN;
304       capture_config.path = NULL;
305       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
306       capture_config.loop = false;
307     } else {
308       log_info("Using media file: %s (webcam disabled)", media_file_val);
309       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
310       capture_config.path = media_file_val;
311       capture_config.loop = GET_OPTION(media_loop);
312 
313       if (fps_explicitly_set) {
314         capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)user_fps;
315         log_info("Using user-specified FPS: %u", capture_config.target_fps);
316       } else {
317         // Always probe FPS for local files, even in snapshot mode
318         // (snapshot mode needs correct FPS to capture right number of frames during snapshot_delay window)
319         log_debug("Probing FPS for local file");
320         probe_source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, media_file_val);
321         if (probe_source) {
322           double file_fps = media_source_get_video_fps(probe_source);
323           log_info("Detected file video FPS: %.1f", file_fps);
324           if (file_fps > 0.0) {
325             capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)(file_fps + 0.5);
326           } else {
327             log_warn("FPS detection failed, using default 60 FPS");
328             capture_config.target_fps = 60;
329           }
330         } else {
331           log_warn("Failed to create probe source for FPS detection, using default 60 FPS");
332           capture_config.target_fps = 60;
333         }
334       }
335     }
336   } else if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
337     // Test pattern
338     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Using test pattern");
339     log_info("Using test pattern");
340     capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
341     capture_config.path = NULL;
342     capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
343     capture_config.loop = false;
344   } else {
345     // Default: Try webcam if stdout is a TTY, otherwise use test pattern
346     // (webcam + piped output often fails, test pattern is reliable fallback)
347     bool stdout_is_tty = terminal_is_stdout_tty();
348     if (stdout_is_tty) {
349       // Normal terminal mode: use webcam
350       log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Stdout is TTY, using local webcam");
351       log_info("Using local webcam");
352       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM;
353       capture_config.path = NULL;
354       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
355       capture_config.loop = false;
356     } else {
357       // Piped output mode: use test pattern (webcam + pipes often fails)
358       log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Stdout is piped, defaulting to test pattern for reliability");
359       log_info("Stdout is piped - using test pattern instead of webcam");
360       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
361       capture_config.path = NULL;
362       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
363       capture_config.loop = false;
364     }
365   }
366 
367   // Apply initial seek if specified
368   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_seek_timestamp option");
369   capture_config.initial_seek_timestamp = GET_OPTION(media_seek_timestamp);
370   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Media seek timestamp set");
371 
372   // Clean up probe source (don't reuse for actual capture)
373   if (probe_source) {
374     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Destroying probe source");
375     media_source_destroy(probe_source);
376     probe_source = NULL;
377     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Probe source destroyed");
378   } else {
379     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): No probe source to destroy");
380   }
381 
382   // Pass probed FPS to display context for correct render-file duration
383   if (capture_config.target_fps > 0) {
384     session_display_set_render_fps(display, capture_config.target_fps);
385     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Set display render FPS to %u for correct video encoding duration",
386               capture_config.target_fps);
387   }
388 
389   // ============================================================================
390   // SETUP: Network Transports (TCP/WebSocket)
391   // ============================================================================
392 
393   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Setting up network transports");
394 
395   bool networked_mode = session_client_like_is_networked(config->kind);
396 
397   switch (config->kind) {
398     case SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR:
399       log_debug("Mirror mode detected - will use local capture with media source");
400       break;
401     case SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT:
402       log_debug("Client/Network mode detected - will use network capture without local media source");
403       break;
404     case SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY:
405       log_debug("Discovery mode detected - discovery session will manage networking");
406       break;
407   }
408 
409   // ============================================================================
410   // SETUP: Capture Context
411   // ============================================================================
412 
413   // Check for stdin render mode: read ASCII frames from stdin, render to video
414   // Only enable stdin render mode if:
415   // 1. render-file is "-" (output to stdout)
416   // 2. stdin is not a TTY
417   // 3. --height is explicitly specified (user must opt-in to stdin render mode)
418   // 4. NO media source is being used (not reading from file/URL/test-pattern)
419   // This prevents accidental activation when piping to other processes or using mirror mode
420 #ifndef _WIN32
421   const char *render_file_opt = GET_OPTION(render_file);
422 #else
423   const char *render_file_opt = NULL;
424 #endif
425   int frame_height = GET_OPTION(height);
426   bool height_explicitly_set = (frame_height != OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT);
427   bool has_media_source = (media_url_val && strlen(media_url_val) > 0) ||
428                           (media_file_val && strlen(media_file_val) > 0) ||
429                           GET_OPTION(test_pattern);
430 
431   // Enable audio capture if render-file is active and media source exists
432   if (render_file_opt && strlen(render_file_opt) > 0 && has_media_source) {
433     capture_config.enable_audio = true;
434     log_debug("Audio capture enabled for render-file encoding");
435   }
436 
437   bool stdin_render_mode = (render_file_opt && strcmp(render_file_opt, "-") == 0 &&
438                             !terminal_is_stdin_tty() && height_explicitly_set &&
439                             !has_media_source && config->kind != SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR);
440 
441   if (stdin_render_mode) {
442     // Stdin render mode: read ASCII frames from stdin, output video to stdout
443     log_info("Stdin render mode enabled: reading ASCII frames from stdin, output to stdout");
444 
445     // Get frame dimensions (--height must be explicitly set to reach here)
446     int frame_width = GET_OPTION(width);
447 
448     // Validate stdin render mode constraints (--height is already validated above since height_explicitly_set is true)
449     bool width_was_explicitly_set = (frame_width != OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT);
450 
451     if (width_was_explicitly_set) {
452       result = SET_ERRNO(ERROR_USAGE, "Stdin render mode does not accept --width (auto-detected from frames).\n"
453                                       "Only specify: --height <rows>");
454       goto cleanup;
455     }
456 
457     asciichat_error_t stdin_err = terminal_fd_reader_create(STDIN_FILENO, frame_height, &g_stdin_reader);
458     if (stdin_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
459       log_fatal("Failed to initialize stdin frame reader: %s", asciichat_error_string(stdin_err));
460       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
461       goto cleanup;
462     }
463 
464     // For stdin render mode, create a minimal capture context for compatibility
465     int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
466     capture = session_network_capture_create((uint32_t)(fps > 0 ? fps : 60));
467     if (!capture) {
468       log_fatal("Failed to initialize capture context for stdin rendering");
469       terminal_fd_reader_destroy(g_stdin_reader);
470       g_stdin_reader = NULL;
471       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
472       goto cleanup;
473     }
474 
475     log_info("stdin render mode: reading %d-line frames from stdin, auto-detecting width", frame_height);
476   }
477 
478   // Choose capture type based on mode determined earlier:
479   // - Mirror mode: needs to capture local media (webcam, file, test pattern)
480   // - Network modes (client/discovery): receive frames from network, no local capture
481   if (!stdin_render_mode && networked_mode) {
482     // Network mode: create minimal capture context without media source
483     log_debug("Network mode detected - using network capture (no local media source)");
484     int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
485     uint64_t net_cap_start = time_get_ns();
486     capture = session_network_capture_create((uint32_t)(fps > 0 ? fps : 60));
487     double net_cap_ms = time_ns_to_ms(time_elapsed_ns(net_cap_start, time_get_ns()));
488     log_info("★ INIT_CHECKPOINT: Network capture created in %.1f ms", net_cap_ms);
489     if (!capture) {
490       log_fatal("Failed to initialize network capture context");
491       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
492       goto cleanup;
493     }
494     if (fps > 0) {
495       log_debug("Network capture FPS set to %d from options", fps);
496     }
497   }
498 
499   // ============================================================================
500   // SETUP: Audio Context
501   // ============================================================================
502 
503   // Skip audio for immediate snapshots
504   bool should_init_audio = true;
505   if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) == 0.0) {
506     should_init_audio = false;
507     log_debug("Skipping audio initialization for immediate snapshot");
508   }
509 
510   // Probe for audio
511   if (should_init_audio && capture_config.type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE && capture_config.path) {
512     media_source_t *audio_probe_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
513     if (audio_probe_source && media_source_has_audio(audio_probe_source)) {
514       audio_available = true;
515 
516       // Allocate and initialize audio context
517       audio_ctx = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(audio_context_t), audio_context_t *);
518       if (audio_ctx) {
519         *audio_ctx = (audio_context_t){0};
520         if (audio_init(audio_ctx) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
521           // Link audio to media source
522           media_source_t *media_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
523           audio_ctx->media_source = media_source;
524 
525           if (media_source) {
526             media_source_set_audio_context(media_source, audio_ctx);
527           }
528 
529           // Determine if microphone should be enabled
530           bool should_enable_mic = audio_should_enable_microphone(GET_OPTION(audio_source), audio_available);
531           audio_ctx->playback_only = !should_enable_mic;
532 
533           // Disable jitter buffering for file playback
534           if (audio_ctx->playback_buffer) {
535             audio_ctx->playback_buffer->jitter_buffer_enabled = false;
536             atomic_store_bool(&audio_ctx->playback_buffer->jitter_buffer_filled, true);
537           }
538 
539           // Store in capture for keyboard handler access
540           session_capture_set_audio_context(capture, audio_ctx);
541 
542           log_debug("Audio context initialized");
543         } else {
544           log_warn("Failed to initialize audio context");
545           audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
546           SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
547           audio_ctx = NULL;
548           audio_available = false;
549         }
550       }
551     }
552   }
553 
554   // Pass stdin_reader to display if in stdin render mode
555   if (stdin_render_mode && g_stdin_reader) {
556     session_display_set_stdin_reader(display, g_stdin_reader);
557     log_debug("stdin_reader passed to display context");
558   }
559 
560   // ============================================================================
561   // SETUP: End Splash Screen
562   // ============================================================================
563   log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] About to end splash screen");
564 
565   // The splash screen will display during initialization and exit when done.
566   // The initialization process (media probing, display setup, etc.) naturally
567   // takes time, keeping the splash visible. The splash is kept alive during
568   // connection attempts (TCP and WebSocket) to prevent a blank screen during
569   // network operations. splash_intro_done() is called only after a successful
570   // connection is established.
571 
572   log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Splash will remain visible during connection attempts");
573 
574   // Exit early if shutdown was requested (e.g., user pressed Ctrl-C)
575   if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
576     log_debug("[SETUP] Shutdown requested, exiting early");
577     log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Ending splash due to early shutdown request");
578     splash_intro_done();
579     splash_wait_for_animation();
580     result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
581     goto cleanup;
582   }
583 
584   // Wait for splash animation to finish before proceeding to render loop
585   // (splash_anim thread uses the display, must not be disrupted before cleanup)
586   // But skip for immediate snapshots (snapshot-delay=0) which need to output first frame immediately
587   // For discovery mode, keep the splash running - it stays visible while waiting for peers
588   if (config->discovery == NULL) {
589     splash_intro_done();
590     if (!(GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) == 0.0)) {
591       splash_wait_for_animation();
592     }
593   } else {
594     log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Discovery mode: keeping splash visible while waiting for peers");
595   }
596 
597   // ============================================================================
598   // SETUP: Create Mirror Capture (after splash screen finishes)
599   // ============================================================================
600 
601   log_info("[MIRROR_CHECK] stdin_render_mode=%d, capture=%p", stdin_render_mode, (void *)capture);
602   if (!stdin_render_mode && !capture) {
603     // Mirror mode: create capture context with local media source (NOW, after splash finishes)
604     log_info("Mirror mode detected - creating mirror capture after splash screen");
605     uint64_t mirror_cap_start = time_get_ns();
606     capture = session_mirror_capture_create(&capture_config);
607     double mirror_cap_ms = time_ns_to_ms(time_elapsed_ns(mirror_cap_start, time_get_ns()));
608     log_info("★ INIT_CHECKPOINT: Mirror capture created in %.1f ms (after splash)", mirror_cap_ms);
609     if (!capture) {
610       log_fatal("Failed to initialize mirror capture source");
611       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
612       goto cleanup;
613     }
614 
615     // Set audio source for render-file encoding (after capture is created)
616     media_source_t *render_audio_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
617 #ifndef _WIN32
618     const char *render_file_path = GET_OPTION(render_file);
619 #else
620     const char *render_file_path = NULL;
621 #endif
622     log_info("[AUDIO_SOURCE] render_audio_source=%p, render_file=%s, render_file_len=%zu",
623              render_audio_source, render_file_path ? render_file_path : "(null)",
624              render_file_path ? strlen(render_file_path) : 0);
625     if (render_audio_source && render_file_path && strlen(render_file_path) > 0) {
626       session_display_set_render_audio_source(display, render_audio_source);
627       log_info("Audio source set for render-file output");
628     } else {
629       log_warn("[AUDIO_SOURCE] Conditions not met: render_audio_source=%p, render_file_path=%s, len=%zu",
630                render_audio_source, render_file_path ? render_file_path : "(null)",
631                render_file_path ? strlen(render_file_path) : 0);
632     }
633   }
634 
635   // ============================================================================
636   // SETUP: Start Audio Playback
637   // ============================================================================
638 
639   log_debug("[SETUP_AUDIO] About to check audio context for duplex");
640   if (audio_ctx && audio_available) {
641     log_debug("[SETUP_AUDIO] Starting audio duplex");
642     if (audio_start_duplex(audio_ctx) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
643       log_info("Audio playback started");
644     } else {
645       log_warn("Failed to start audio duplex");
646       audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
647       SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
648       audio_ctx = NULL;
649       audio_available = false;
650     }
651   }
652 
653   log_info("[MARKER] Audio setup complete at line 614");
654   log_info("[SETUP_COMPLETE] All setup complete, about to start connection loop");
655 
656   // ============================================================================
657   // RUN: Mode-Specific Main Loop with Reconnection
658   // ============================================================================
659 
660   // Connection/attempt loop - wraps run_fn with reconnection logic
661   int attempt = 0;
662   int max_attempts = config->max_reconnect_attempts;
663 
664   log_info("[CLIENT_LIKE_LOOP] Starting connection loop, max_attempts=%d", max_attempts);
665 
666   while (true) {
667     attempt++;
668 
669     result = config->run_fn(capture, display, config->run_user_data);
670 
671     // Exit immediately if run_fn succeeded
672     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
673       // Connection succeeded - splash screen cleanup now happens when first frame renders
674       // This ensures the splash stays visible during TCP/WebSocket/datachannel connection attempts
675 
676       break;
677     }
678 
679     // Check if we should attempt reconnection
680     bool should_retry = false;
681 
682     if (max_attempts != 0) { // max_attempts == 0 means no retries
683       // Check custom reconnect logic if provided
684       if (config->should_reconnect_callback) {
685         should_retry = config->should_reconnect_callback(result, attempt, config->reconnect_user_data);
686       } else {
687         should_retry = true; // Default: always retry
688       }
689 
690       // Check retry limits if still planning to retry
691       if (should_retry && max_attempts > 0 && attempt >= max_attempts) {
692         log_debug("Reached maximum reconnection attempts (%d), giving up", max_attempts);
693         should_retry = false;
694       }
695     }
696 
697     // If not retrying or shutdown requested, exit loop
698     if (!should_retry || APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
699       break;
700     }
701 
702     // Reconnection will happen - show splash screen during reconnection attempt
703     // Reset first_frame flag so splash cleanup runs on next successful connection
704     session_display_reset_first_frame(display);
705 
706     // splash_intro_start() handles all the logic:
707     // - If splash should display: shows splash and disables console logging
708     // - If splash shouldn't display: restores stderr and returns without changing logging state
709     splash_intro_start(display);
710 
711     // Log reconnection message
712     if (max_attempts == -1) {
713       log_info("Connection failed, retrying...");
714     } else if (max_attempts > 0) {
715       log_info("Connection failed, retrying (attempt %d/%d)...", attempt + 1, max_attempts);
716     }
717 
718     // Apply reconnection delay if configured
719     // Check APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit) frequently during sleep so SIGTERM can interrupt reconnection attempts
720     if (config->reconnect_delay_ms > 0) {
721       unsigned int remaining_ms = config->reconnect_delay_ms;
722       const unsigned int check_interval_ms = 100; // Check exit flag every 100ms
723 
724       while (remaining_ms > 0 && !APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
725         unsigned int sleep_ms = (remaining_ms < check_interval_ms) ? remaining_ms : check_interval_ms;
726         APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
727         APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT(platform_sleep_ms, sleep_ms);
728         remaining_ms -= sleep_ms;
729       }
730     }
731 
732     // Continue loop to retry
733   }
734 
735   // ============================================================================
736   // CLEANUP (always runs, even on error)
737   // ============================================================================
738 
739 cleanup:
740   // Re-enable terminal output for shutdown logs
741   log_set_terminal_output(true);
742 
743   g_current_config = NULL;
744   g_render_should_exit = NULL;
745 
746   // CRITICAL: Terminate PortAudio device resources FIRST
747   log_debug("Terminating PortAudio device resources");
748   audio_terminate_portaudio_final();
749 
750   // Stop audio thread before destroying audio context to prevent use-after-free
751   // The audio worker thread may still be logging when we destroy the buffer
752   APP_CALLBACK_VOID(audio_stop_thread);
753 
754   // Stop and destroy audio (after PortAudio is terminated and audio thread is stopped)
755   if (audio_ctx) {
756     audio_stop_duplex(audio_ctx);
757     audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
758     SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
759     audio_ctx = NULL;
760   }
761 
762   // Destroy display (check global context to see if discovery mode already destroyed it)
763   // Discovery participant mode may destroy the display early and clear the global context
764   session_display_ctx_t *current_global = session_display_get_global_context();
765   if (display && display == current_global) {
766     // Display is still valid (not destroyed by discovery mode)
767     session_display_destroy(display);
768     display = NULL;
769   } else if (display && display != current_global) {
770     // Display was already destroyed (by discovery participant mode)
771     // Just NULL it out, don't call destroy again
772     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Display was already destroyed by discovery mode, skipping destroy");
773     display = NULL;
774   }
775 
776   // Destroy capture
777   if (capture) {
778     session_capture_destroy(capture);
779     capture = NULL;
780   }
781 
782   // Destroy stdin reader if active (stdin render mode)
783   if (g_stdin_reader) {
784     terminal_fd_reader_destroy(g_stdin_reader);
785     g_stdin_reader = NULL;
786   }
787 
788   // Clean up probe source if still allocated (shouldn't happen, but be safe)
789   if (probe_source) {
790     media_source_destroy(probe_source);
791     probe_source = NULL;
792   }
793 
794   // Stop splash animation and enforce minimum display time (even on error path)
795   // But skip completely if shutting down - don't interact with splash at all during shutdown
796   // The animation thread will exit on its own when it detects shutdown_is_requested()
797   // For discovery mode, keep the splash running - it stays visible while waiting for peers
798   if (config->discovery != NULL) {
799     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Discovery mode: keeping splash alive for peer waiting UI");
800   } else if (!APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
801     log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call splash_intro_done()");
802     splash_intro_done();
803     log_debug("[CLEANUP] splash_intro_done() returned");
804 
805     // Wait for animation thread to exit before cleanup
806     log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call splash_wait_for_animation()");
807     splash_wait_for_animation();
808     log_debug("[CLEANUP] splash_wait_for_animation() returned");
809   } else {
810     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Skipping all splash operations (shutdown in progress)");
811     // During shutdown, don't interact with splash - let animation thread exit naturally
812     // and don't wait for it (prevents blocking on signals)
813   }
814 
815   // Stop debug sync thread before destroying log buffer to prevent use-after-free
816   log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call debug_sync_cleanup_thread()");
817   debug_sync_cleanup_thread();
818   log_debug("[CLEANUP] debug_sync_cleanup_thread() returned");
819 
820   // Disable keepawake (re-allow OS to sleep)
821   log_debug("[CLEANUP] Disabling keepawake");
822   platform_disable_keepawake();
823   log_debug("[CLEANUP] keepawake disabled");
824 
825   // Write newline to separate final frame from prompt (if configured)
826   log_debug("About to check print_newline_on_tty_exit");
827   if (config->print_newline_on_tty_exit && terminal_is_stdout_tty()) {
828     log_debug("Writing newline");
829     const char newline = '\n';
830     (void)platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, &newline, 1);
831   }
832 
833   log_set_terminal_output(false);
834 
835   return result;
836 }






References APP_CALLBACK_BOOL, APP_CALLBACK_VOID,
    APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_store_bool(), audio_destroy(), audio_init(),
    audio_should_enable_microphone(), audio_start_duplex(),
    audio_stop_duplex(), audio_stop_thread(),
    audio_terminate_portaudio_final(),
    session_capture_config_t::callback_data,
    session_display_config_t::callback_data,
    session_display_config_t::color_mode,
    session_display_config_t::custom_palette,
    debug_sync_cleanup_thread(),
    session_client_like_config::discovery,
    session_capture_config_t::enable_audio, ERROR_DISPLAY,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, ERROR_USAGE,
    GET_OPTION, session_capture_config_t::initial_seek_timestamp,
    audio_ring_buffer::jitter_buffer_enabled,
    audio_ring_buffer::jitter_buffer_filled,
    session_client_like_config::kind, log_debug, log_fatal,
    log_info, log_set_force_stderr(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), log_warn,
    session_capture_config_t::loop,
    session_client_like_config::max_reconnect_attempts,
    audio_context_t::media_source, media_source_create(),
    media_source_destroy(), MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE,
    media_source_get_video_fps(), media_source_has_audio(),
    media_source_set_audio_context(), MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN,
    MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST, MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM,
    OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT, OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT,
    session_display_config_t::palette_type,
    session_capture_config_t::path, platform_disable_keepawake(),
    platform_enable_keepawake(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    platform_write_all(), audio_context_t::playback_buffer,
    audio_context_t::playback_only,
    session_client_like_config::print_newline_on_tty_exit,
    session_client_like_config::reconnect_delay_ms,
    session_client_like_config::reconnect_user_data,
    session_capture_config_t::resize_for_network,
    session_client_like_config::run_fn,
    session_client_like_config::run_user_data, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, session_capture_destroy(),
    session_capture_get_media_source(),
    session_capture_set_audio_context(),
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT,
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY,
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR, session_display_create(),
    session_display_destroy(),
    session_display_get_global_context(),
    session_display_reset_first_frame(),
    session_display_set_global_context(),
    session_display_set_render_audio_source(),
    session_display_set_render_fps(),
    session_display_set_stdin_reader(),
    session_mirror_capture_create(),
    session_network_capture_create(), SET_ERRNO,
    should_exit(), session_capture_config_t::should_exit_callback,
    session_display_config_t::should_exit_callback,
    session_client_like_config::should_reconnect_callback,
    session_display_config_t::snapshot_mode, splash_intro_done(),
    splash_intro_start(), splash_restore_stderr(),
    splash_wait_for_animation(),
    session_capture_config_t::target_fps, TERM_COLOR_AUTO,
    terminal_fd_reader_create(), terminal_fd_reader_destroy(),
    terminal_is_stdin_tty(), terminal_is_stdout_tty(),
    terminal_should_force_stderr(), time_elapsed_ns(),
    time_get_ns(), and session_capture_config_t::type.


Referenced by client_main(), discovery_main(), and
    mirror_main().








Variable
  Documentation




bool(*)(void *)
  session_client_like_get_render_should_exit(void) (void )


Get the render loop should_exit callback set by
    session_client_like_run().




This is used by mode-specific run_fn callbacks to obtain the
    proper should_exit callback for passing to session_render_loop(). The
    callback checks both the global should_exit() flag and any
    mode-specific custom_should_exit condition.




Returns


Function pointer to the render should_exit callback, or
  NULL if session_client_like_run() has not been called yet.


Definition at line 245 of file client_like.h.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.19
    ascii-chat
